methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
REGENT Craft is a Series A-stage transportation company founded by Billy Thalheimer and Mike Klinker that has raised $60M to date. The transportation sector comp universe includes eight private peers—notably Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Pipedream, and FleetZero—though no public comparables were identified. No same-stage or adjacent-stage peers with sufficient data density exist to support peer-median valuation triangulation.

Given data constraints, stage-progression methodology is the applicable approach. Using the $60M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $120M. This method assumes REGENT Craft's progression follows standard venture step-up patterns and that current capital raised reasonably proxies pre-money valuation at this stage.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $102M (low) to $138M (high), centered on $120M. The wide range reflects reliance on a single methodological pillar and the absence of direct peer benchmarking. Key uncertainties include the actual pre-money valuation at Series A close, capital efficiency and burn trajectory relative to sector norms, and competitive positioning within an emerging transportation technology landscape where comparables remain sparse and stage-heterogeneous. Additional diligence on unit economics and market traction would narrow this range.
